name: YouTube Data MCP Server
what_it_does: YouTube Data API MCP server enabling AI agents to access YouTube video and channel data — searching videos, retrieving video metadata and statistics, fetching channel information, getting video captions/transcripts, and integrating YouTube's data into agent-driven content research, video analysis, and social media analytics workflows.
use_cases: Searching YouTube for videos on specific topics from content research agents
Retrieving video statistics (views, likes, comments) from analytics agents
Getting video transcripts for content analysis from summarization agents
Analyzing channel performance and growth from social media agents
Finding competitor video strategies from market research agents
Tracking trending videos and topics from content strategy agents
not_for: Downloading YouTube videos (violates ToS; use legal alternatives)
Teams needing Vimeo, TikTok, or other video platforms
High-frequency polling (YouTube API has strict quota limits)
best_when: An agent needs YouTube video data — metadata, statistics, transcripts, or search results — for content research, analytics, or social media intelligence.
avoid_when: You need to download video files, access private/premium content, or require real-time streaming data.

auth.notes: YouTube Data API v3 key for public data (videos, channels, search). OAuth 2.0 required for user-specific data (subscriptions, playlists). API key available from Google Cloud Console.

pricing.free_tier_limits: YouTube Data API: 10,000 quota units/day free
pricing.paid_tiers: Additional quota via Google Cloud billing (rare need for moderate use)
pricing.notes: YouTube Data API v3 has 10,000 free quota units/day. Different operations cost different units (search costs 100 units). Free tier is generally sufficient for moderate agent use.

performance.rate_limits: 10,000 quota units/day on free tier; search costs 100 units per call

known_agent_gotchas: YouTube API quota is measured in 'units' not requests — search is expensive (100 units)
Daily quota resets at midnight Pacific Time — agents must handle quota exhaustion gracefully
Transcript retrieval requires youtube-transcript-api or similar; not in YouTube Data API directly
OAuth 2.0 required for user data — API key only gives access to public data
Community MCP — may not expose full YouTube Data API surface (comments, live streams, etc.)
YouTube may change API quotas or deprecate endpoints without warning
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key for public data; OAuth for user data. GDPR compliant. Community MCP. Store API key as environment variable. Restrict API key to YouTube Data API in Google Cloud Console.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key with YouTube-restricted scope. OAuth for user data. GDPR. Restrict API key to YouTube Data API only.
